title: Pound Cake Gets Its Pound of Fame
story: Every March 4, bakers and dessert lovers celebrate National Pound Cake Day by whipping up this classic treat! Traditional pound cake got its name because recipes once called for a whole pound each of butter, sugar, eggs, and flour. Today, people bake lighter versions and get creative with flavors like lemon, vanilla, or chocolate chip. Many families share their favorite pound cake recipes on social media or host baking parties to mark this sweet holiday.
activity_question: Why was pound cake originally called "pound cake"?
activity_choices: Because it weighed exactly one pound when finished
Because recipes used a pound each of four main ingredients
Because it cost one pound of money to make
Because you had to pound the batter for an hour
activity_answer: Because recipes used a pound each of four main ingredients
bonus_fact: Some pound cakes today use cream cheese or sour cream for extra moisture!

title: Hugs for Heroes Day
story: Every March 4, Americans celebrate National Hug a GI Day by showing appreciation for military service members. title: A Legendary Cartoonist's Colorful Beginning
story: Pencils ready! On March 4, 1933, David Sutherland was born in Invergordon, Scotland. He grew up to become a famous cartoonist, bringing laughter to countless children. Sutherland's playful drawings brought characters like Dennis the Menace and the Bash Street Kids to life in the popular comic magazine Beano. His creative spirit and funny illustrations delighted readers for decades.
activity_question: What comic magazine did David Sutherland draw for?
follow_up_question: Can you name one of the characters David Sutherland drew?

title: The Man Behind 'Oh Yeah'
story: Zürich welcomed a creative powerhouse on March 4, 1945. Dieter Meier, born that day, would grow up to wear many artistic hats. He became famous as the singer of Yello, the band behind the catchy song 'Oh Yeah'. But Meier's talents didn't stop at music. He also made a name for himself as a thought-provoking conceptual artist and an actor.
activity_question: What song is Dieter Meier famous for singing?
follow_up_question: Besides music, what other two artistic fields did Dieter Meier work in?

title: Vermont Joins the United States
story: Green mountains and maple syrup! Vermont became the 14th state to join the United States in 1791. On March 4, it made history as the first new state added after the original 13 colonies. Vermont brought its beautiful forests, friendly people, and delicious maple treats to the growing nation.
activity_question: What number state did Vermont become when it joined the United States?
bonus_fact: Vermont's name comes from French words meaning 'green mountain.'

title: Sweet Surprise Arrives in Hawaii
story: Juicy, tangy oranges rolled onto Hawaiian shores for the first time in 1792. Captain George Vancouver brought these sunny fruits as a gift on March 4. Hawaiians had never tasted anything like them before! The oranges were a hit, and soon trees were planted all over the islands. Today, Hawaii grows many delicious citrus fruits.
activity_question: Who brought oranges to Hawaii?
bonus_fact: Oranges aren't native to Hawaii - they came from Southeast Asia!

title: Darwin's Ship Sails into Chilean Waters
story: Waves crashed against the hull as HMS Beagle glided into the Bay of Concepcion, Chile. It was March 4, 1835, and the ship carried a young naturalist named Charles Darwin. His observations on this voyage would later inspire his groundbreaking ideas about evolution. The Beagle's journey opened Darwin's eyes to the wonders of nature around the world.
activity_question: What was the name of the ship that sailed into the Bay of Concepcion?
bonus_fact: The Beagle's voyage lasted nearly five years, circling the globe and visiting many exotic locations.

title: Hockey Heroes Claim the Cup
story: Cheers erupted as the Montreal Hockey Club skated to victory in 1903. On March 4, they faced off against the Winnipeg Victorias in a thrilling match. The Montreal team clinched the Stanley Cup with a 4-1 win, sealing their challenge series triumph. This exciting showdown took place at the Montreal Arena in Westmount, Quebec.
activity_question: Where did the Stanley Cup match take place?
bonus_fact: The Stanley Cup is the oldest trophy in North American professional sports.

title: Bubbling Up: America's Steamy National Park
story: Steaming waters and healing springs! In 1921, Hot Springs became America's smallest national park. Located in Arkansas, it protects ancient thermal springs once used by Native Americans. On March 4 that year, this unique area was officially preserved, allowing visitors to soak in its warm, mineral-rich waters and explore the surrounding Ouachita Mountains.
activity_question: What does Hot Springs National Park protect?
bonus_fact: The park's springs can reach temperatures of up to 143°F!

title: Anne Boleyn's Royal Debut
story: A dazzling young woman stepped into the spotlight at the English court. It was Anne Boleyn, making her grand entrance at the Green Castle pageant on March 4, 1522. Little did anyone know that this charming newcomer would one day capture a king's heart and change the course of history.
activity_question: Where did Anne Boleyn make her debut?
follow_up_question: What role did Anne Boleyn later play in English history?
